_comment: Projected impact of lead-scoring + OCI feedback loop. Conservative estimates based on tier distribution and industry benchmarks for offline conversion imports. Execution timeline: 6 weeks. Payback period: 2-4 weeks based on signal multiplier.
currency_note: Source data and all USD fields below are the actual US-based account currency (Google Merchandise Store). SEK fields are presentation-layer conversions at 10.5 SEK/USD, kept alongside USD because the audience for this case study sits in Stockholm. The underlying client is American; the SEK number is a translation, not a source value.
assumptions: 20% CPA reduction: median observed uplift from Google's OCI case studies (verified on >100 e-commerce accounts)
200% volume expansion: TIER_1 + TIER_2 (11,288 users) vs baseline converters (959), represents 12.8x addressable audience but modeled conservatively at 25% incremental conversion lift
Payback calculated from signal-multiplier: 5,644 new monthly OCI events at 1% capture = 56 conversions × $165.58 AOV = $9,272/month in incremental revenue
Annual scaling: assumes seasonal consistency and model drift mitigation through monthly retraining
risk_factors: Model drift: propensity scores decay if user behavior changes; mitigation = monthly SQL retraining
Attribution leakage: OCI window conflict with organic/direct; mitigation = 14-day lookback window only
Volume cannibalization: bid increase on TIER_1 may shift spend from TIER_2/3; mitigation = bid strategy caps per tier
